content.full_text:
A-81-02-007

TRANSMITTAL OF CORRESPONDENCE
DATE
CORRESPONDENT Robert K. Baker, c/o Capt. Hir Miggs REC'D. 2/20
DATED 2/19 RE: Campaign Reporting

ADMINISTRATION CONFLICTS/LEGAL ENFORCEMENT
BOB T. JOH S.
LINDA STELLA ROGER
BEV

EXECUTIVE BARBARA
TOM H. DIANE TAMA
TED PATSY LYNN
NANCY W. CARLA
JEANNE MARYANN
BOB S. MERMA
JOHN

INFORMATION REQUEST FOR ADVICE--MUST BE ANSWERED WITHIN 21 DAYS

PLEASE:
TAKE WHATEVER ACTION YOU CONSIDER APPROPRIATE
REPLY DIRECT BY 5/1 -COPY TO [illegible]
PREPARE [illegible] KEEP AND RETURN TO
COORDINAN...LY WITH

3/2/81 Alfred Robert Baker —

1) The campaign statement filed after Dec 31, 1979 failed to include
expenditure from 1/1/79 to 6/30/79 in the cumulative amount. Mr.
Baker will file amended to correct the cumulative expenditure
for all 1979 reports.

2) Campaign statement filed in 1979 & 1980 disclosed cash-on-hand
which expense was requested according. I instructed Mr.
Baker to file amendments declaring zero (0) cash values. We do
discussed the method to simplify the reporting of contribution from .
previously dne

JLC

2/19/81

Northern California Retail Liquor Dealers Association

2705 K STREET, SUITE 6 • SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95816 • PHONE (916) 444-9807

February 19, 1981


Fair Political Practices Commission,
Technical Assistance & Analysis Division
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

A-81-02-009

Re: California Retail Liquor Dealers
Association, North I.D. # 790492

Gentlemen:

Help!!!

I have just, by virtue of a resignation and a discharge, fallen heir to the
Recipient Committee reporting responsibility for the "Committee" above-designated.

I have reviewed the 1979 and 1980 calendar year filings of the "Committee",
and it appears that all political donations, contributions and expenditures have,
in fact, been reported. I am troubled, however, by two aspects of the filings
of record, and seek your guidance.

I. "CUMULATION PERIOD", "POST-ELECTION STATEMENTS."

It appears that a semi-annual campaign statement [form 450] for July 1-
December 31, 1979, was filed, and not treated as a post-election statement. The
January 1, 1980-April 24, 1980 statement [form 450] reported incorrectly the
"cumulative expenditures from prior statement", item IV, #7, and such error has
been reiterated with each subsequent filing.

Assuming (and, with no disrespect, I don't pretend I can understand the
instructions or explanations set forth in your manual(s)) that this "committee"
must cumulate its receipts and expenditures through each two-year session of the
Legislature, filing "Post-Election statements" only at the conclusion of even-
numbered years, please advise what, if any documents must now be filed to correct
the "carry-forward" error in our 1980 filings.

II. "Receipts"

This organization is a California non-profit mutual benefit corporation,
sustained by membership dues, only one of the functions of which is on-line
activity. To my knowledge no funds specifically earmarked, appropriated, or
solicited for political activity have ever existed. The Board of Directors
invariably budgets a sum for political contributions, and pending availability
of such sum from general revenues or funds, authorizes campaign, or other,
political contributions.


BUZZ FIEDLER              DAVID DOLT              ALLEN CALLEON              DAVID EUSARO          LYLE D. JONES
President                Vice President          Secretary/Treasurer        Legislative Advocate    Past President

It appears that, in the past the personnel preparing forms 450 have treated the figures of fifteen ($15.00) dollars for the "first stores" and seven and one-half (7.50) for "second stores" as "contributions received" for purposes of form 450, although there is no basis, within the articles, by-laws, or minutes of the corporation, for such allocation. In fact, all campaign or political contributions have been made form common funds of the corporation, and in fact, political expenditures in 1979 and 1980 were significantly less than the "contributions" reported, while normal operating expenses, including political contributions, nearly exhausted revenue. Thus, I find myself "holding the bag" with a post election form 450 showing $8,602.00 "cash on hand" which doesn't truly exist as a fund. More corporate funds, in fact, exist, but are not allocated to political activity.

I assume the proper method for an organization such as this one to report "contribution income" would be periodic resolution to allocate general funds to a political contribution fund, for immediate disbursement, so that no appearance of an earmarked or allocated fund by falsely presented. Please advise if such procedure is proper, and how I correct 1980's filings to accurately reflect the true situation, while complying with the law.

Finally, I assume that the final form 450, covering the period 10-21--12-31-80, constitutes a "post-election" statement for this "committee", and that 1981 statements, commencing with the semi-annual statement for the 1st half of 1981, will "start fresh" from a standpoint of both expenditures and contributions. Please advise if such assumption is incorrect.

Despairingly yours,

[signature]
Robert K. Baker
Executive Director

RKB:sac

embedding.qa_text: This document is a transmittal of correspondence dated 3/19, addressed to Robert K. Baker, No. Calif. Retail Liquor Dealers Assoc., regarding campaign reporting. The document lists various administrators and legal contacts, with some names circled or marked as "RICH" or "HELEN-L.A." The sender requests advice and states that whatever action is considered appropriate should be taken. The handwritten notes below the typed section read: 3/2/81 Called Robert Baker. 1) The campaign statements filed after Dec 31, 1979 failed to include expenditures from 1/1/79 to 6/30/79 in the cumulative amount. Mr. Baker will file amendments to correct the cumulative expenditure for all 1980 reports. 2) Campaign statements filed for 1979 & 1980 disclosed cash-on-hand which is erroneous because of interval accounting. I instructed Mr. Baker to file amendments disclosing zero (0) cash balances. We also discussed two methods to simplify the reporting of contributions from membership dues. J.R.S. It appears that a semi-annual campaign statement [form 450] for July 1-December 31, 1979, was filed, and not treated as a post-election statement. The January 1, 1980-April 24, 1980 statement [form 450] reported incorrectly the "cumulative expenditures from prior statement", item IV, #7, and such error has been reiterated with each subsequent filing.
